machinery_genes.quote: A distinguishing feature of gene organization in [...] T3SS clusters is a split gene coding for the outer membrane secretin protein SctC, i.e. a HrcC/YscC homologue
machinery_genes.note: Gazi et al 2012 Table 1 (unified Sct nomenclature) lists HrcC in the Hrp-Hrc2 / Ralstonia solanacearum row at the SctC secretin position; the body text quoted here confirms HrcC as the YscC-family outer-membrane secretin. R. solanacearum-specific naming.

machinery_genes.quote: the protein building the inner ring of the T3SS basal body SctJ (RhcJ/HrcJ/YscJ homolog)
machinery_genes.note: Gazi et al 2012; HrcJ named at the SctJ inner-ring position in the Ralstonia solanacearum row of Table 1, and the body text identifies the HrcJ/YscJ homolog as the inner-ring basal-body protein.

machinery_genes.quote: the T3SS ATPase protein SctN (RhcN/HrcN/YscN/FliI homolog)
machinery_genes.note: Gazi et al 2012; HrcN listed at the SctN ATPase position in the Ralstonia solanacearum row of Table 1; body text names the HrcN/YscN/FliI homolog as the T3SS ATPase.

machinery_genes.quote: the two T3SS gate proteins SctU and SctV (RhcU/HrcU/YscU/FlhB and RhcV/HrcV/LcrD/FlhA homologs respectively)
machinery_genes.note: Gazi et al 2012; HrcV at the SctV export-apparatus position in the Ralstonia solanacearum row of Table 1; body text names the HrcV/LcrD/FlhA homolog as a core export-gate protein.

machinery_genes.quote: the two T3SS gate proteins SctU and SctV (RhcU/HrcU/YscU/FlhB and RhcV/HrcV/LcrD/FlhA homologs respectively)
machinery_genes.note: Gazi et al 2012; HrcU at the SctU position in the Ralstonia solanacearum row of Table 1; YscU/FlhB-family export-gate autoprotease.

machinery_genes.quote: the three core membrane proteins SctR, SctS, SctT (RhcRST/HrcRST/YscRST/FliPQR homologs)
machinery_genes.note: Gazi et al 2012; HrcR at the SctR position in the Ralstonia solanacearum row of Table 1; body text names HrcRST as the three core membrane export-gate proteins.

machinery_genes.quote: the three core membrane proteins SctR, SctS, SctT (RhcRST/HrcRST/YscRST/FliPQR homologs)
machinery_genes.note: Gazi et al 2012; HrcS at the SctS position in the Ralstonia solanacearum row of Table 1.

machinery_genes.quote: the three core membrane proteins SctR, SctS, SctT (RhcRST/HrcRST/YscRST/FliPQR homologs)
machinery_genes.note: Gazi et al 2012; HrcT at the SctT position in the Ralstonia solanacearum row of Table 1.

machinery_genes.quote: the protein building the cytoplasmic ring SctQ (RhcQ/HrcQ/YscQ/FliY homolog)
machinery_genes.note: Gazi et al 2012; HrcQ at the SctQ position in the Ralstonia solanacearum row of Table 1; body text names the HrcQ/YscQ/FliY homolog as the cytoplasmic-ring protein.

machinery_genes.quote: Phylogenetic analyses of the core T3SS proteins [...] SctN (RhcN/HrcN/YscN/FliI and homologues)
machinery_genes.note: Gazi et al 2012 Table 1 places HrpD at the SctO (YscO/FliJ family, ATPase stalk) column of the Ralstonia solanacearum row. Lower-confidence assignment: the per-gene quote available is the table cell rather than a body sentence; the SctO/HrpO stalk family is described in the body but under the generic Sct names.

machinery_genes.role: Hrp pilus subunit (extracellular appendage / needle equivalent)
machinery_genes.quote: hrp genes are also involved in the biogenesis of pili that are mainly composed of the HrpY protein
machinery_genes.note: Van Gijsegem et al 2000 characterized the R. solanacearum GMI1000 Hrp pilus and showed it is built mainly of HrpY; an hrpY mutant is defective in pilus production and PopA secretion. HrpY occupies the SctF needle/pilus-subunit position in Gazi et al 2012 Table 1.

machinery_genes.quote: Considerable sequence similarities were found between HrpO and eight known or predicted prokaryotic proteins: LcrD of Yersinia pestis and Y. enterocolitica, FlbF of Caulobacter crescentus, FlhA of Bacillus subtilis, MxiA and VirH of Shigella flexneri, InvA of Salmonella typhimurium and HrpC2 of Xanthomonas campestris pv. vesicatoria
machinery_genes.note: Gough et al 1993 sequenced part of the GMI1000 hrp cluster and named HrpO. NOTE NOMENCLATURE: this 1993 'HrpO' is homologous to the LcrD/MxiA/InvA/FlhA (SctV) export-apparatus family per the quote, distinct from the later 'SctO/YscO' stalk usage of the letter O. Listed as an apparatus export-machinery component on the strength of these homologies; named in GMI1000 specifically.

machinery_genes.quote: The corresponding putative proteins, HrpN, HrpO and HpaP, have predicted sizes of 357, 690 and 197 amino acids [...] HrpN and HrpO are both predicted to be hydrophobic proteins with potential membrane-spanning domains
machinery_genes.note: Gough et al 1993; HrpN named as a GMI1000 hrp-cluster ORF whose product is a predicted membrane-spanning hydrophobic protein. No explicit Sct-family assignment in the literature accessed; lower-confidence machinery call (kept because it is a named, mutation-relevant hrp-cluster membrane component, not an effector). NOTE: R. solanacearum HrpN is unrelated to the Erwinia 'harpin' HrpN.

machinery_genes.role: required for Hrp pilus assembly (apparatus biogenesis)
machinery_genes.quote: we also provide evidence that hrpV and hrpX mutants can still target the HrpY pilin outside the bacterial cell but are impaired in the production of Hrp pili, indicating that HrpV and HrpX proteins are involved in the assembly of this appendage
machinery_genes.note: Van Gijsegem et al 2002 genetic dissection of the GMI1000 hrp cluster; HrpV required for assembly of the Hrp pilus. Apparatus-biogenesis component (not an effector or regulator).

machinery_genes.role: required for Hrp pilus assembly (apparatus biogenesis)
machinery_genes.quote: we also provide evidence that hrpV and hrpX mutants can still target the HrpY pilin outside the bacterial cell but are impaired in the production of Hrp pili, indicating that HrpV and HrpX proteins are involved in the assembly of this appendage
machinery_genes.note: Van Gijsegem et al 2002; HrpX required for Hrp pilus assembly in GMI1000.

machinery_genes.role: type III translocator (host-membrane pore for effector delivery)
machinery_genes.quote: We conclude that PopF1 and PopF2 are type III translocators belonging to the HrpF/NopX family.
machinery_genes.note: Meyer et al 2006, R. solanacearum GMI1000; PopF1 required for translocation of AvrA into host cells. Translocators are apparatus (host-membrane pore), not effectors, per the brief. Renamed RipF1_1 in later nomenclature.

machinery_genes.role: type III translocator (host-membrane pore for effector delivery)
machinery_genes.quote: We conclude that PopF1 and PopF2 are type III translocators belonging to the HrpF/NopX family.
machinery_genes.note: Meyer et al 2006, R. solanacearum GMI1000; PopF2 type III translocator (renamed RipF1_2 later). Apparatus (host-membrane pore), not an effector.

notes: System: the Hrp (HR and pathogenicity) type III secretion system of Ralstonia solanacearum GMI1000, a >20-gene hrp/hrc cluster on the megaplasmid that injects Rip/Pop effectors into plant cells and elicits the HR on non-hosts; a member of the plant-pathogen Hrp-Hrc2 (Sct) injectisome family. The foundational apparatus papers are Van Gijsegem et al 1995 (sequenced the cluster, ~20 Hrp proteins, 8 homologous to Ysc/Spa/Mxi/Fli secretion/flagellar components) and Gough et al 1993 (GMI1000 hrp sequence; named HrpN, HrpO, HpaP). Core Sct/Hrc apparatus genes (HrcC secretin, HrcJ inner ring, HrcN ATPase, HrcV export-apparatus, HrcU autoprotease gate, HrcR/HrcS/HrcT export gates, HrcQ C-ring/sorting platform, HrpD SctO stalk) are taken from the published unified-nomenclature Table 1 of Gazi et al 2012, which lists these gene names explicitly in the Hrp-Hrc2 / Ralstonia solanacearum row (R.-solanacearum-specific naming, not a model-strain transfer). The Hrp pilus subunit HrpY is from Van Gijsegem 2000; the pilus-assembly factors HrpV and HrpX from Van Gijsegem 2002; the translocators PopF1/PopF2 (later RipF1_1/RipF1_2) from Meyer et al 2006. Deliberately EXCLUDED: the regulators HrpB, HrpG, PrhA, PrhI/PrhR, PrhJ (transcriptional cascade); the chaperones HpaB, HpaD; HpaP (named in Gough 1993 but its mutation has no plant-interaction phenotype, i.e. not required); HrpW (a glycine-rich harpin/pectate-lyase, secreted accessory, not core apparatus); and all secreted Rip/Pop effectors (PopA/PopB/PopC, AvrA, and the curated RSc effectors). Low-confidence calls flagged in their notes: HrpD (per-gene quote is the Gazi Table cell, not a body sentence), HrpN (named hrp-cluster membrane protein with no explicit Sct family assignment), and the HrpO nomenclature caveat (1993 HrpO is an SctV/LcrD-family export-apparatus protein, not the later SctO stalk). I did not mine the RefSeq GenBank; locus-tag resolution (RSp/RSc) is deferred to the scripted step. STRING (267608) externally associates hrcV with RSp0863 and hrcR with RSp0860, consistent with a megaplasmid hrp cluster, but I did not use these as naming evidence. All quotes are verbatim from PubMed metadata abstracts or the PMC full text (Gazi 2012, PMC3574062), and every DOI/PMID was confirmed via get_article_metadata.
